description: Pairwise preference elicitation pool. Human-eval build plan Session 31. Each pair is a hand-picked side-by-side judgement. When `expected_preferred` is set, that side is the author's best answer; responses aligning with it feed the auto-annotator's precedent index as a second signal source alongside approved annotations. Pairs without an `expected_preferred` are genuine probes — we record responses but don't score alignment.

pairs.prompt: Which confirms a destructive action more clearly?
pairs.left_text: Delete your account. This can't be undone.
pairs.right_text: Are you sure you want to continue?
pairs.rationale: PRF-01 asks confirmations to name the action and its consequences. The left option does both; the right is generic.

pairs.right_text: Please confirm your request to opt out of our weekly digest communication.
pairs.rationale: GRM-02 discourages filler verbiage in confirmations.

pairs.prompt: Which welcome headline sets a clearer expectation?
pairs.left_text: Run design copy past a second pair of eyes.
pairs.right_text: Welcome aboard! We're so excited to have you.
pairs.rationale: TN-03 prefers concrete descriptions of what a product does over generic greetings.

pairs.prompt: Which error message helps the user recover?
pairs.left_text: We couldn't save your draft. Check your connection and try again.
pairs.rationale: HLP-01 requires errors to name the failure plus a recovery path. Codes-only messages fail.

pairs.prompt: Which login error is more honest?
pairs.left_text: That password doesn't match. Try again or reset it.
pairs.right_text: Your email or password is incorrect.
pairs.rationale: PRF-02 warns against user-enumerating errors — the ambiguous 'email or password' is the safer pattern.

pairs.prompt: Which empty state is more useful?
pairs.left_text: No messages yet. When someone replies, you'll see it here.
pairs.rationale: CLR-02 asks empty states to set expectations for what will appear and when.

pairs.prompt: Which confirmation gives the user what they need next?
pairs.left_text: Order confirmed. We'll email a receipt to you@example.com within 10 minutes.
pairs.right_text: Thank you! Your order has been successfully placed.
pairs.rationale: CLR-01 prefers confirmations that tell you what happens next over generic gratitude.
TN-01 prefers the word users actually search for.

pairs.prompt: Which permission prompt gives informed consent?
pairs.left_text: Allow ContentRX to read your clipboard so you can paste copy into scans?
pairs.rationale: PRM-01 asks permission prompts to state what will happen with the permission and why.

pairs.prompt: Which cookie banner is more forthright?
pairs.left_text: We use cookies to keep you signed in and remember settings. Third-party analytics cookies are off by default.
pairs.right_text: We value your privacy. We use cookies to enhance your experience.
pairs.rationale: TRA-02 requires specificity — naming categories over vague 'enhance your experience' framing.

pairs.prompt: Which search placeholder is more helpful?
pairs.left_text: Search by title, tag, or project
pairs.rationale: HLP-02 asks placeholders to describe searchable dimensions when there's more than one.

pairs.prompt: Which upgrade CTA is more concrete?
pairs.right_text: Take your workflow to the next level
pairs.rationale: GRM-01 prefers concrete CTAs that state what you get and what it costs.

pairs.prompt: Which save button label is clearer?
pairs.rationale: TN-02 prefers verbs that match the user's mental model of what's happening.

pairs.left_text: Your first scan is done — three issues flagged in TN category.
pairs.rationale: TN-03 warns against gushing praise that doesn't convey information.

pairs.prompt: Which payment trust copy reads more honest?
pairs.left_text: We use Stripe to process payments. We never store your card number.
pairs.right_text: Your information is safe with us.
pairs.rationale: TRA-01 prefers named partners and concrete commitments over 'trust us' generalities.
